history.rationale: The revision adds tighter instructions to distinguish direct evidence from speculation, tailor output to document-list and timeline questions, include exact document identifiers when relevant, and avoid overstating unsupported conclusions such as mental state, conspiratorial findings, or inferred relationships.

This revision tightens instructions against unsupported inference, requires explicit handling of sparse timelines and cross-document associations, and emphasizes naming exact documents for document-identification questions while preserving the original structure and citation rules.

The revision tightens rules for partial evidence, timelines, relationship claims, and document-identification answers so the model reports only explicit links and named records instead of inferring motives, associations, or stronger conclusions than the retrieved documents support.

The revision adds explicit guardrails for document-identification, timelines, association claims, support-for-conclusion questions, and mental-state questions so the model sticks to exact retrieved evidence, names concrete documents, and avoids assembling unsupported links or generic filler.

The revision adds targeted instructions for document-list, timeline, relationship, conclusion, and mental-state questions so the model stays anchored to explicit document-level evidence, avoids combining separate references into unsupported links, and produces partial but concrete answers instead of generic summaries.

The revision adds explicit guards against inferred linkages, mislabeled interview/timeline entries, and overclaiming from single-source allegations while strengthening document-identification requirements for cross-reference and partial-evidence questions.
